title: Zuri's New Friend
synopsis: Zuri feels nervous on her first day at a new playground, but with encouragement from her mom and her own kind heart, she discovers that making friends can be as simple as sharing and caring for others.

pages.text: Zuri held her mom's hand tightly as they walked to the new playground. Her flower clip sparkled in the morning sun. "I don't know anyone here, Mama," she whispered.

illustration.description: Zuri with natural coily hair in an afro with a flower clip, deep brown skin, bright eyes walking hand-in-hand with her mother toward a bright colorful playground with swings and slides in the background, other diverse children playing
illustration.alt: Zuri holding her mother's hand while approaching a sunny playground

pages.text: "That's okay, sweetie," Mama said with a warm smile. "Sometimes the best friendships start with just saying hello. You have such a kind heart, Zuri."

illustration.description: Close-up of Zuri with natural coily hair in an afro with a flower clip, deep brown skin, bright eyes looking up at her smiling mother, bright primary colors and white space around them
illustration.alt: Zuri looking up at her encouraging mother

pages.text: Zuri watched the other children laugh and play. There were kids on the swings, going high up in the air. Others were sliding down the big yellow slide. Everyone looked like they were having so much fun.

illustration.description: Wide view of the playground with diverse children playing on swings and slides, Zuri with natural coily hair in an afro with a flower clip, deep brown skin, bright eyes standing at the edge watching, bright primary colors and joyful energy
illustration.alt: Children playing happily on playground equipment while Zuri watches

pages.text: Just then, Zuri heard someone crying. Near the sandbox, a little boy sat alone. His knee was scraped and he looked very sad. All the other kids kept playing, but Zuri felt worried about him.

illustration.description: Zuri with natural coily hair in an afro with a flower clip, deep brown skin, bright eyes looking concerned at a sad little boy sitting by a sandbox with a scraped knee, other children playing in the background, bright playground setting
illustration.alt: Zuri noticing a crying boy with a scraped knee by the sandbox

pages.text: Zuri remembered what Mama always said: "When someone needs help, a true friend steps forward." She took a deep breath and walked over to the boy. "Hi, I'm Zuri. Are you okay?"

illustration.description: Zuri with natural coily hair in an afro with a flower clip, deep brown skin, bright eyes approaching the sad boy, extending her hand in friendship, sunny playground with primary colors and white space
illustration.alt: Zuri introducing herself to the injured boy

pages.text: The boy looked up with teary eyes. "I'm Marcus. I fell down and hurt my knee. It really stings!" Zuri sat down beside him. "My mama has a band-aid in her purse. Want me to get it for you?"

illustration.description: Zuri with natural coily hair in an afro with a flower clip, deep brown skin, bright eyes sitting next to Marcus in the sandbox, showing kindness and care, bright primary colors and inclusive playground scene
illustration.alt: Zuri sitting beside Marcus, offering to help with his injury

pages.text: After Mama helped clean Marcus's knee and put on a colorful band-aid, he smiled big. "Thank you, Zuri! You're really nice. Do you want to be friends?"

illustration.description: Zuri with natural coily hair in an afro with a flower clip, deep brown skin, bright eyes and Marcus both smiling, with Zuri's mother in the background, bright playground setting with primary colors and joyful energy
illustration.alt: Zuri and Marcus smiling together after getting help from Zuri's mom

pages.text: "Yes!" Zuri said happily. "I was nervous about making friends today, but helping you made me feel brave." Marcus grinned. "Well, you're the bravest and kindest friend I could ask for!"

illustration.description: Zuri with natural coily hair in an afro with a flower clip, deep brown skin, bright eyes and Marcus holding hands and laughing together, surrounded by the bright sunny playground with swings and slides, primary colors and white space
illustration.alt: Zuri and Marcus holding hands and celebrating their new friendship

pages.text: Soon, other children came over to meet Zuri too. They all played together on the swings and slides. Zuri's flower clip bobbed as she laughed. She learned that being a good friend starts with caring about others.

illustration.description: Group scene with Zuri with natural coily hair in an afro with a flower clip, deep brown skin, bright eyes playing with Marcus and several other diverse children on playground equipment, everyone laughing and having fun, bright primary colors and inclusive friendship activities
illustration.alt: Zuri playing happily with her new group of friends on the playground

pages.text: When it was time to go home, Zuri hugged her new friends goodbye. "Thank you for teaching me something important today," Mama said. "Kindness is the best way to make friends." Zuri smiled. She couldn't wait to come back tomorrow.

illustration.description: Zuri with natural coily hair in an afro with a flower clip, deep brown skin, bright eyes waving goodbye to her friends while walking away with her mother, sunny playground in background with other children waving back, bright joyful colors
illustration.alt: Zuri and her mother leaving the playground while her new friends wave goodbye

metadata.illustrationStyle: bright sunny playground, diverse kids playing, primary colors, white space, joyful energetic, inclusive, park swings slides, laughter, friendship activities

questions.question: How did Zuri feel when she first arrived at the playground?
questions.explanation: Zuri felt nervous because she didn't know anyone at the new playground.

questions.question: What happened to Marcus?
questions.explanation: Marcus fell down and scraped his knee, which made him cry.

questions.question: How did Zuri help Marcus?
choices: She got him a band-aid from her mom
questions.explanation: Zuri asked her mama to get a band-aid to help with Marcus's scraped knee.

questions.question: What did Mama say about making friends?
choices: Kindness is the best way to make friends
Only talk to kids you know
questions.explanation: Mama told Zuri that kindness is the best way to make friends.

questions.question: How did Zuri feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: Zuri was happy about her new friends and couldn't wait to come back to the playground tomorrow.
